evals.prompt: Write tests for `gptase/models/types.py` -- cover all the Pydantic models and multimodal content types (TextContent, ImageUrlContent). I want to make sure defaults and validators are covered.
evals.expected_output: A test file at tests/test_models.py (or additions to it) with class-based tests for each type. No @pytest.mark.asyncio decorators. Tests run green with conda run -n llm pytest.

evals.prompt: The `gptase/sop/failure_handler.py` has an async method `decide_on_failure` that has no unit tests. Add tests for it without making any real API calls.
evals.expected_output: Tests in tests/test_sop.py or a new file, mocking the LLM layer via AsyncMock, verifying the method's decision logic. No real API calls. Tests pass with conda run.

evals.prompt: I just added a `get_agent_names()` function in `gptase/agents/loader.py` that returns a list of agent names from the registry. Write the tests for it.
evals.expected_output: Tests in tests/test_agents/ directory following project conventions. Uses fixtures or mocks appropriately. Green pytest output shown.
